title: notifications core — entitlements
description: Aggregate entitlements resolved at `/auth/me` → `data.quota_summary`. Actual per-channel usage counters live in the respective channel module. Core aggregates them for a single-pane UX and enforces the aggregate caps (e.g. custom-templates.count applies platform-wide).

reference.distinctFrom: features.json (binary capability toggles) — entitlements are countable

kinds.description: Countable slots (custom-templates.count). One-per-row consumption. Refused with 402 when at cap.
Consumable pool (mail.month, sms.month, push.month). Decremented on use, reset on period roll.
Feature toggle expressed as an entitlement (digest.enabled).
'No cap' for a normally-slotted resource. `remaining` null on the wire.

entitlements.description: Monthly outbound mail cap per workspace. Reset at UTC month boundary. Rolls forward to next month's send after 3 warning notifications when approaching cap.
Monthly outbound SMS cap per workspace. SMS incurs per-message cost so cap is tighter than mail.
Monthly outbound push cap. FCM + APNs are free-per-message so cap is generous.
Max concurrent workspace-override templates. Platform defaults don't count. Reached cap → workspace must archive a template before creating another.
Whether digest batching is available to workspace users. Currently on for every plan; kept as an entitlement so future plans can toggle off if user-count grows unbounded on free plan.
Hot-retention window for Notification rows before cold-archive + eventual purge. Higher plans keep hot data longer for compliance / support use cases.
